fields.name: Unique Key
fields.label: Record ID
fields.description: A surrogate primary key – a unique integer that identifies each complaint record.

fields.name: Created Date
fields.label: Complaint Created Date
fields.description: The calendar date when the complaint was first entered into the system.

fields.name: Closed Date
fields.label: Complaint Closed Date
fields.description: The calendar date on which the complaint case was resolved or closed by the agency.

fields.name: Agency
fields.label: Handling Agency (abbreviation)
fields.description: Two‑ to four‑letter code identifying the agency that processed the complaint (e.g., NYPD, DOT).

fields.name: Agency Name
fields.label: Handling Agency (full name)
fields.description: The full official name of the agency responsible for the complaint.
fields.examples:
New York City Police Depa… [265038]
Department of Housing Pre… [258019]
Department of Transportat… [132462]
Other… [103974]
Department of Environment… [75895]

fields.name: Complaint Type
fields.label: Primary Complaint Category
fields.description: Broad category describing the nature of the complaint, such as noise or parking issues.
fields.examples:
Other… [563561]
Noise - Residential [89439]
HEAT/HOT WATER [56639]
Illegal Parking [45032]
Blocked Driveway [42356]

fields.name: Descriptor
fields.label: Complaint Descriptor
fields.description: A more specific label that refines the primary complaint type (e.g., "Loud Music/Party" under Noise).
fields.examples:
Other… [671870]
Loud Music/Party [93646]
ENTIRE BUILDING [36885]
HEAT [35088]
No Access [31631]

fields.name: Location Type
fields.label: Physical Location Category
fields.description: Classification of the place where the incident occurred (e.g., residential building, street).
fields.examples:
RESIDENTIAL BUILDING [255562]
(NULL)… [239131]
Street/Sidewalk [145653]
Residential Building/Hous… [92765]
Street [92190]

fields.name: Incident Zip
fields.label: Incident ZIP Code
fields.description: Five‑digit ZIP code for the area where the complaint was reported.

fields.name: Incident Address
fields.label: Full Incident Address
fields.description: Street address or intersection describing the exact location of the incident. Includes street number, name and directional prefixes when available.
fields.examples:
Other… [819046]
(NULL)… [174700]
655 EAST  230 STREET [1538]
78-15 PARSONS BOULEVARD [694]
672 EAST  231 STREET [663]

fields.name: Street Name
fields.label: Primary Street Name
fields.description: The main street involved in the incident; a component of the full Incident Address.
fields.examples:
Other… [784684]
(NULL)… [174720]
BROADWAY [9702]
GRAND CONCOURSE [5851]
OCEAN AVENUE [3946]

fields.name: Cross Street 1
fields.label: First Cross Street
fields.description: First cross‑street intersecting at the incident location; part of the address context.

fields.name: Cross Street 2
fields.label: Second Cross Street
fields.description: Second cross‑street intersecting at the incident location, if applicable.

fields.name: Intersection Street 1
fields.label: First Intersection Street
fields.description: One of the streets that form an intersection at the incident site; used when Address Type = INTERSECTION.

fields.name: Intersection Street 2
fields.label: Second Intersection Street
fields.description: The other street forming the intersection at the incident site.

fields.name: Address Type
fields.label: Address Entry Type
fields.description: Code indicating how the address was entered (e.g., ADDRESS, INTERSECTION, LATLONG).

fields.name: City
fields.label: Incident City / Borough
fields.description: Name of the city or borough where the incident took place.

fields.name: Landmark
fields.label: Nearby Landmark
fields.description: Notable landmark or feature close to the incident location; may be a public building, park, or other point of interest.
fields.examples:
(NULL)… [912779]
Other… [80165]
EAST  230 STREET [1545]
EAST  231 STREET [1291]
BROADWAY [1148]

fields.name: Facility Type
fields.label: Related Facility Category
fields.description: Type of facility involved in the complaint (e.g., garage, school district).

fields.name: Status
fields.label: Complaint Status
fields.description: Current state of the complaint workflow (e.g., Open, Pending, Closed).
fields.enumeration:
Assigned
Closed
Closed - Testing
Email Sent
In Progress
Open
Pending
Started
Unassigned
Unspecified
fields.examples:
Closed [952522]
Pending [20119]
Open [12340]
In Progress [7841]
Assigned [6651]

fields.name: Due Date
fields.label: Resolution Deadline
fields.description: Date and time by which the agency is expected to resolve or complete action on the complaint.

fields.name: Resolution Description
fields.label: Resolution Narrative
fields.description: Free‑text summary of actions taken, outcomes, or follow‑up instructions for the complaint.
fields.min: A DOB violation was issued for failing to comply with an existing Stop Work Order.
fields.max: Your request was submitted to the Department of Homeless Services. The City?s outreach team will assess the homeless individual and offer appropriate assistance within 2 hours. If you asked to know the outcome of your request, you will get a call within 2 hours. No further status will be available through the NYC 311 App, 311, or 311 Online.
fields.examples:
Other… [511739]
The Police Department res… [91408]
The Department of Housing… [72962]
The Police Department res… [63868]
Service Request status fo… [52155]

fields.name: Resolution Action Updated Date
fields.label: Last Resolution Update Date
fields.description: Date on which the most recent resolution action was recorded.

fields.name: Community Board
fields.label: Community Board Identifier
fields.description: Code and name of the community board that has jurisdiction over the incident area.

fields.name: BBL
fields.label: Borough‑Block‑Lot (BBL)
fields.description: Ten‑digit code uniquely identifying a NYC property parcel; used for spatial referencing.

fields.name: Borough
fields.label: Incident Borough
fields.description: Name of the borough where the incident occurred.
fields.enumeration:
BRONX
BROOKLYN
MANHATTAN
QUEENS
STATEN ISLAND
Unspecified

fields.name: X Coordinate (State Plane)
fields.label: State‑Plane X Coordinate
fields.description: Horizontal coordinate in the New York State Plane system for the incident location.

fields.name: Y Coordinate (State Plane)
fields.label: State‑Plane Y Coordinate
fields.description: Vertical coordinate in the New York State Plane system for the incident location.

fields.name: Open Data Channel Type
fields.label: Submission Channel
fields.description: Medium through which the complaint was submitted (e.g., PHONE, ONLINE, MOBILE).

fields.name: Park Facility Name

fields.label: Geographic Latitude
fields.description: Decimal‑degree latitude of the incident location, used for spatial analysis.

Description.response:
**Description**

The dataset is a one‑million‑record sample of New York City 311 service requests collected between January 2010 and December 2020. Each record represents an individual complaint, uniquely identified by the surrogate key *Unique Key*. The primary temporal fields—*Created Date* and *Closed Date*—span roughly a decade, with most complaints created in 2013‑2015 and closed shortly thereafter. Complaint content is encoded through several hierarchical categories (*Agency*, *Complaint Type*, *Descriptor*) and geospatial identifiers (*Latitude/Longitude*, *Borough*, *BBL*). The data also includes administrative metadata such as the submitting channel, resolution narrative, and community board jurisdiction.

**Notable Characteristics**

- **Temporal concentration**: Created dates cluster around 2013‑2015 (≈34 % of records) with a mild negative skew (-0.017), indicating a slight leftward tail in complaint volume.
- **High cardinality & uniqueness**: Latitude/Longitude and BBL have >35,000 unique values each; <ALL_UNIQUE> entries dominate the frequency tables, implying fine‑grained spatial granularity but sparse categorical support for modeling.
- **Dominant “Other” categories**: For *Complaint Type* (~56 % of cases) and *Descriptor* (~67 %) the value “Other” absorbs a large share of complaints, signalling poor classification coverage and potential data quality issues.
- **Missingness patterns**:  
  - *Closed Date*: 28,619 nulls (≈2.9 %).  
  - *Incident Zip*, *Address Type*, *Due Date*: 55k–126k nulls each (~5‑12 %).  
  - *Resolution Description* has 20,480 nulls (≈2 %).
- **Duplicate handling**: The surrogate primary key ensures no duplicate records; however, many fields exhibit duplicate values across records (e.g., Agency codes, borough names), which is expected for categorical data.
- **Outliers & placeholders**: *Closed Date* min/max values (1900‑01‑01 / 2100‑01‑01) appear to be placeholder defaults rather than real dates; these should be treated as missing or censored.  
  Similarly, *Latitude/Longitude* extremes (e.g., –77.5196, –73.7006) correspond to boundary coordinates of the NYC metro area.
- **PII / PHI**: The dataset contains street addresses, ZIP codes, and latitude/longitude pairs that can be used to re‑identify individuals or properties; it should not be shared without appropriate de‑identification or aggregation.
